revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鼻節神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hanabushi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hanabushi Shrine.jpg | location = 1 Dare-no-michi, Hanabuchi-hama, Shichigahama Town, Miyagi District, Miyagi Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|38.296061|N|141.084817|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | Classification = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}), Former {{ill|Mura-sha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mura-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} Subordinate Shrine | Established = Traditional date: 630 CE (Shōmei 2) | Architecture = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | Festival = {{ill|September 28 (lunar calendar)|ja|Lunisolar September28日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 28 (lunar calendar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | prefecture= {{ill|Shichigahama|qid=Q478272|1=Shichigahama}} | architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }}
'''Hanabushi Shrine''' (鼻節神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Shichigahama|en|Shichigahama|qid=Q478272|lt=Shichigahama Town|1=Shichigahama}}, Miyagi District, Miyagi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was historically classified as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}. The shrine is situated on Tarumi-yama, at the eastern end of the Shichigahama Peninsula, overlooking the Sendai Bay. It is venerated as a guardian deity of maritime safety.
== Deities == * {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3090037}} – deity of crossroads and guidance.
The name "Hanabushi" is said to derive from Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto's prominent nose, which was marked with a distinctive ridge ("bushi")<ref>'Hanabushi Daimyōjin no Goengi,' cited in the 'Miyagi District Gazetteer.'</ref>.
== History == === Establishment === According to shrine tradition, the deity worshiped here landed at Hanabushi Beach in the Age of the Gods. Initially enshrined at "Hōga Saki," the shrine was moved to its current location on Tarumi-yama in 770 CE due to strong winds repeatedly damaging the structures at the original site.
=== Recognition in Ancient Records === The shrine was elevated to {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in 844 CE for its miraculous efficacy, as noted in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}. It was listed as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} in 927 CE.
=== Cultural and Historical Significance === Hanabushi Shrine is referenced in classical literature, such as the {{ill|The Pillow Book|en|The Pillow Book|qid=Q615137|lt=The Pillow Book|1=The Pillow Book}}, and was renowned among Kyoto's elite as a notable shrine<ref name="meikan">Miyagi Shrine Directory.</ref>. In the Heian period, the shrine received imperial envoys and was associated with purification rituals.
In the Kamakura period, the local Hanabuchi clan provided patronage. Over time, the shrine became a subordinate shrine of {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama Shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}}.
== Cultural Properties == === Local Cultural Assets === * '''Kokufu-Kuriya Seal (国府厨印)''': A bronze seal discovered in the shrine grounds in 1868. It is believed to have been used for administrative purposes in the ancient Taga Provincial Headquarters. Currently preserved at the Shichigahama Town Historical Museum.
=== Other Notable Features === The shrine is associated with the submerged "Daikon Reef," believed to be the original shrine site, which was submerged during the 869 CE Jōgan Earthquake and Tsunami.
== Access == '''Address:''' * 1 Dare-no-michi, Hanabuchi-hama, Shichigahama Town, Miyagi District, Miyagi Prefecture
'''Transportation:''' * JR East {{ill|Senseki Line|en|Senseki Line|qid=Q1134623|lt=Senseki Line|1=Senseki Line}}: Accessible via bus from Tagajō, Shimomachi, or Hon-Shiogama Stations.
'''Nearby Landmarks:''' * Hanabuchi Lighthouse * Matsushima Bay * Shichigahama International Village
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020358 Hanabushi Shrine – Miyagi Shrine Bureau] * [http://www.shichigahama.com/relax2/spot05.html Hanabushi Shrine – Shichigahama Town Official Site] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270901.html Hanabushi Shrine – Kokugakuin University Shrine Database]
{{translated page|ja|鼻節神社|version=100320667|comment=Imported full ja history}}
